name: Pork Collioure Stew
recipeIngredient: 4 cups All-purpose broth (or low-sodium chicken broth)
4 Sprigs fresh thyme (or 1/2 tsp Dried thyme)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Pat pork dry with paper towels, then sprinkle with desired salt and pepper.
3. Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat on the stove.
4. Add pork (without crowding) and brown well on all sides. Work in batches if needed. Remove pieces to a plate as they are brown; pour off all but about 2 tablespoons of fat.
5. Reduce heat to low, replace the pot on the stove, and add onion. Cook for 5 minutes, scraping up any brown bits that adhere to the pan.
6. Add flour and cook another minute, stirring.
7. Add wine, vinegar, and broth; bring to a boil. Return pork (with juices) to the pot along with bay leaves and thyme. Cover tightly and transfer to the oven.
8. Cook for 1 hour and 15 minutes, or until meat is barely tender.
9. Remove casserole from the oven; using a slotted spoon, remove meat to a plate.
10. Strain sauce through a fine sieve into a container; discard onions and herbs. Return meat to the pot.
11. Add horseradish, mustard, cream, lemon juice, and parsnips. Cover and return to the oven.
12. Cook for 20-30 minutes, or until meat is tender and parsnips are cooked.
13. Serve with boiled potatoes or buttered noodles.
